prompt: Which of these formulas is a contradiction (false in every row of its truth table)?
hint: A contradiction can never be true. Which formula asserts something AND its own negation?
explanation: P ∧ ¬P claims P and not-P at once, which is impossible, so it is false in every row — a contradiction. P ∨ ¬P, P → P, and P ↔ P are all tautologies (always true).
